title: adapter.py: queue_from_radio uses MAX_QUEUE_SIZE but logs confusing message
description:
File: python/src/lichen/interface/meshtastic/adapter.py
Lines: 264-291

The queue_from_radio function has inconsistent queue size constants:

```python
MAX_QUEUE_SIZE = 64  # Defined at module level
MAX_TX_QUEUE = 8     # Also defined

def queue_from_radio(self, data: bytes) -> bool:
    with self._lock:
        if not self.connected:
            return False
        queue_len = len(self.from_radio_queue)
        if queue_len >= MAX_QUEUE_SIZE:  # Uses 64
            log.warning("FromRadio queue full (%d), rejecting message", queue_len)
            return False
        if queue_len >= QUEUE_WARN_THRESHOLD:  # 48
            log.warning("FromRadio queue near capacity (%d/%d)", queue_len, MAX_QUEUE_SIZE)
```

The function logs MAX_QUEUE_SIZE (64) but elsewhere MAX_TX_QUEUE (8) is used for flow control. This is confusing - are there two different queues with different limits?

Also, the warning says 'near capacity' but then 'queue full' at the same threshold in different code paths.

Impact: Confusing log messages; unclear relationship between MAX_QUEUE_SIZE and MAX_TX_QUEUE.

Fix: Clarify the relationship between these constants and ensure consistent naming/usage.
